title: NPTEL IoT 2024 JAN

questions.question: What is the full form of IoT?
questions.detailed_solution: The full form of IoT is "Internet of Things"
questions.reference: See lecture 1 (Introduction to IoT - Part - I) @ 1:30

questions.question: Which of the following technologies have unified and has resulted in the evolution of IoT?
questions.detailed_solution:
Unification of technologies which has resulted in the advancement of
IoT are -
a. Low-power embedded systems
b. Cloud Computing
c. Big Data
d. Machine Learning
e. Networking
questions.reference: See lecture 1 (Introduction to IoT - Part - I) @ 5:54

questions.question: Which of the following are the enablers of IoT?
questions.detailed_solution:
The enables of IoT are -
a. RFID
b. Nanotechnology
c. Sensors
d. Smart Networks
questions.reference: See lecture 2 (Introduction to IoT - Part - I) @ 12:50

questions.question: Which of the following is/are a function of an IoT Gateway?

options.text: Connects the IoT LAN to a WAN
Can implement several LAN and WAN
Forwards packets between LAN and WAN on the IP layer

questions.detailed_solution:
The functionalities of an IoT Gateway are -
a. Connects the IoT LAN to a WAN
b. Can implement several LAN and WAN
c. Forwards packets between LAN and WAN on the IP layer
questions.reference: See lecture 2 (Introduction to IoT - Part - II) @ 3:09

questions.question: State whether the following statement is True or False. Statement: "The same address of an IoT device may be repeated in the domain of another gateway. The gateway has a unique network prefix, which can be used to identify them globally."
questions.detailed_solution:
The same address of an IoT device may be repeated in the domain of
another gateway. The gateway has a unique network prefix, which can be used to identify
them globally.
questions.reference: See lecture 2 (Introduction to IoT - Part - II) @ 07:00

questions.question: In _______________ a node/network is connected to multiple networks for improved reliability.
questions.detailed_solution:
In multi-homing, a node/network is connected to multiple networks for
improved reliability.
questions.reference: See lecture 2 (Introduction to IoT - Part - II) @ 15:22

questions.question: The IPv4 notation uses ___________________ number of bits to represent an address.
questions.detailed_solution: The IPv4 notation uses 32 bits to represent an address.
questions.reference: See lecture 2 (Introduction to IoT - Part - II) @ 16:33

questions.question: The "Source Address" field in the IPv6 header is of __________ bits in length.
questions.detailed_solution: The IPv6 header uses 128 bits to represent the "Source Address".
questions.reference: See lecture 2 (Introduction to IoT - Part - II) @ 18:03

questions.question: A device which detects or measures a physical property and records, indicates, or otherwise responds to it is called _________________?
questions.detailed_solution:
A device which detects or measures a physical property and records,
indicates, or otherwise responds to it is called a sensor.

questions.question: A microphone is an example of an _____________.
questions.detailed_solution: A microphone is an example of an input device and transducer.

questions.question: State whether the following statement is True or False. Statement: A sensor is only sensitive to the measured property for which it has been made (e.g., A temperature sensor senses the ambient temperature of a room.)
questions.detailed_solution:
A sensor is only sensitive to the measured property (e.g., A temperature
sensor senses the ambient temperature of a room.)
.

questions.question: Based on the data type, sensors are classified as _______________________.
questions.detailed_solution:
Based on the data type, sensors are classified as Scalar and
Vector/Multimedia.

questions.question: State whether the following statement is True or False. Statement: The more the resolution of a sensor, the less accurate its precision.
questions.detailed_solution: The more the resolution of a sensor, the more accurate its precision.

questions.question: The sensitivity of a sensor under real conditions may differ from the value specified. This is called ____________________.
questions.detailed_solution:
The sensitivity of a sensor under real conditions may differ from the value
specified.

questions.question: A sensor node is made up of which of the following?
questions.detailed_solution:
A sensor node is made up of a combination of sensor/sensors, a processor
unit, a radio unit, and a power unit.
questions.reference: See Page number - 101, Chapter - 5, Book - Introduction to IoT, Authors - Sudip Misra, Anandarup Mukherjee, and Arijit Roy, Publisher - Cambridge University Press, Edition - 1 (2021)

questions.question: The full form of MQTT is
questions.detailed_solution: The full form of MQTT is Message Queue Telemetry Transport.
questions.reference: See lecture 6 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part II) @ 01:51

questions.question: In MQTT, a ________________ controls the publish-subscribe messaging pattern.
questions.detailed_solution:
In MQTT, a message broker controls the publish-subscribe messaging
pattern.
questions.reference: See lecture 6 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part II) @ 03:33

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a Data Protocol in IoT?
questions.detailed_solution:
The data protocols in IoT are -
a) MQTT
b) CoAP
c) AMQP
d) Websocket
questions.reference: See lecture 6 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part II) @ 01:16

questions.question: In MQTT for IoT, the Publishers are ______________.
questions.detailed_solution: In MQTT, the Publishers are lightweight sensors.
questions.reference: See lecture 6 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part II) @ 04:49

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a method in MQTT?
questions.detailed_solution:
Following are the methods in MQTT -
a. Connect
b. Disconnect
c. Subscribe
d. Unsubscribe
e. Publish
questions.reference: See lecture 6 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part II) @ 06:18

questions.question: Which of the following protocols in IoT have been designed for Machine to Machine (M2M) applications such as smart energy and building automation?
questions.detailed_solution:
CoAP has been designed for Machine to Machine (M2M) applications
such as smart energy and building automation.
questions.reference: See lecture 7 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part III) @ 00:41

questions.question: Similar to HTTP, CoAP utilizes which of the following for operation?
questions.detailed_solution:
Similar to HTTP, CoAP utilizes GET, PUT, PUSH, DELETE message
requests to retrieve, create, update, and delete, respectively.
questions.reference: See lecture 7 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part III) @ 08:24

questions.question: What is the full form of XMPP in IoT?
questions.detailed_solution: XMPP stands for Extensible Messaging and Presence Protocol.
questions.reference: See lecture 7 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part III) @ 11:58

questions.question: State whether the following statement if True or False. Statement: XMPP is an open standard protocol.
questions.detailed_solution: XMPP is an open standard protocol.
questions.reference: See lecture 7 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part III) @ 11:58

questions.question: What is the full form AMQP in context of IoT protocol?
questions.detailed_solution: AMQP stands for Advanced Message Queuing Protocol.
questions.reference: See lecture 8 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part IV) @ 0:41

questions.question: What is the basic unit of data in the AMQP protocol?
questions.detailed_solution: In AMQP, the basic unit of data is called a frame.
questions.reference: See lecture 8 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part IV) @ 0:41

questions.question: Which of the following is/are exchange types in AMQP?
questions.detailed_solution:
The AMQP exchange types are -
a) Direct
b) Fan-out
c) Topic
d) Header
questions.reference: See lecture 8 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part IV) @ 08:56

questions.question: IoT networks can be classified in which of the following type/types?
questions.detailed_solution:
The IoT networks can be classified into two types such as
i. Non-Beacon Enabled
ii. Beacon Enabled

questions.question: The OSI model has __________ layers.
questions.detailed_solution:
The OSI model is a conceptual framework that divides any networked
communication system into seven layers.
questions.reference: See Page number - 10, Chapter - 1, Book - Introduction to IoT, Authors - Sudip Misra, Anandarup Mukherjee, and Arijit Roy, Publisher - Cambridge University Press, Edition - 1 (2021)

questions.question: The "Destination Address" in the IPv4 packet represents which of the following?

options.text: The destination node address of the packet
The intermediate hop in the network

text: The destination node address of the packet

questions.detailed_solution:
The "Destination Address" in the IPv4 packet represents the address of
the destination node in the network.
questions.reference: See Page number - 18, Chapter - 1, Book - Introduction to IoT, Authors - Sudip Misra, Anandarup Mukherjee, and Arijit Roy, Publisher - Cambridge University Press, Edition - 1 (2021)
questions.answer_display: The destination node address of the packet

questions.question: State whether the following statement is true or false. Statement: There is no network layer in Wired HART
questions.detailed_solution: There is no network layer in Wired HART.

questions.question: HART operates only in __________ GHz ISM band.
questions.detailed_solution: HART operates only in 2.4 GHz ISM band.

questions.question: Super-frames in HART consist of grouped ___________ wide timeslots.
questions.detailed_solution: Super-frames in HART consist of grouped 10ms wide timeslots.

questions.question: The process of identifying channels consistently affected by interference and removing them from use is known as _________________.
questions.detailed_solution:
Channel Blacklisting identifies channels consistently affected by
interference and removes them from use.

questions.question: State whether the following statement is True or False Statement: Each node is supervised by the application manager and it guides them on when and where to send packets.
questions.detailed_solution:
The Network manager supervises each node in the network and guides
them on when and where to send packets.

questions.question: FeliCa is commonly found in which country?
questions.detailed_solution: FeliCa is commonly found in Japan.

questions.question: State whether the following state is true or false. Statement: NFC tags found in supermarket products are examples of passive NFC.
questions.detailed_solution:
Passive NFC tags found in supermarket products are examples of
passive NFC.

questions.question: Active NFC devices are able to ___________________?
questions.detailed_solution: Active NFC devices are able to collect as well as transmit information.

questions.question: A __________ in NFC emits a small electric current which creates a magnetic field that in turn bridges the physical space between the devices.
questions.detailed_solution:
A reader in NFC emits a small electric current which creates a magnetic
field that in turn bridges the physical space between the devices

questions.question: Bluetooth technology is based on _________________.
questions.detailed_solution: Bluetooth technology is based on Ad-hoc technology.

questions.question: Bluetooth technology operates in the unlicensed industrial, scientific and medical (ISM) band at _________ to ________ GHz.
questions.detailed_solution:
Bluetooth technology operates in the unlicensed industrial, scientific
and medical (ISM) band at 2.4 to 2.485 GHz.

questions.question: Z-wave operates at ________ MHz in the US and ________ MHz in Europe.
questions.detailed_solution: Z-wave operates at 908.42 MHz in the US and 868.42 MHz in Europe.

questions.question: State whether the following statement is true or false. Statement: Sensor nodes have limited battery life.
questions.detailed_solution: Sensors nodes have limited battery life.

questions.question: State whether the following statement is true or false. Statement: The Link Manager Protocol in Bluetooth manages the establishment, authentication, link configuration.
questions.detailed_solution:
The Link Manager Protocol in Bluetooth manages the establishment,
authentication, link configuration.
questions.reference: See Page number - 157, Chapter - 7, Book - Introduction to IoT, Authors - Sudip Misra, Anandarup Mukherjee, and Arijit Roy, Publisher - Cambridge University Press, Edition - 1 (2021)

questions.question: Zigbee commonly uses __________data rate.
questions.detailed_solution: Zigbee commonly uses 250 kbps data rate.
questions.reference: See Page number - 131, Chapter - 7, Book - Introduction to IoT, Authors - Sudip Misra, Anandarup Mukherjee, and Arijit Roy, Publisher - Cambridge University Press, Edition - 1 (2021)

questions.question: State whether the following statement is true or false. Statement: Coverage in WSN is defined as the area-of-interest that is covered satisfactorily.
questions.detailed_solution:
Coverage in WSN is defined as the area-of-interest that is covered
satisfactorily.

questions.question: If transmission range >= 2* sensing range,
questions.detailed_solution:
If transmission range >= 2* sensing range, coverage implies
connectivity.

questions.question: Forest fire monitoring in WSN is an example of which type of reporting?
questions.detailed_solution:
Forest fire monitoring in WSN is an example of event driven
reporting.

questions.question: State whether the following statement is true or false. Statement: Objective of coverage in WSN is to use minimum number of sensors and maximize network lifetime.
questions.detailed_solution:
The objective of coverage in WSN is to use minimum number of
sensors and maximize the network lifetime.

questions.question: What is the full form of AUV in the context of mobile wireless sensor networks?
questions.detailed_solution: The full form of AUV is Autonomous Underwater Vehicle.

questions.question: In mobile wireless sensor networks, the sensor nodes are capable of _____________ and measuring the condition of their surrounding environments.
questions.detailed_solution:
In mobile wireless sensor networks, the sensor nodes are capable of
collaborating with one another and measuring the condition of their surrounding
environments.

questions.question: State whether the following statement is true or false. Statement: In mobile sensor networks, the sensor nodes come in close proximity of sink and deliver the data.
questions.detailed_solution:
In mobile sensor networks, the sensor nodes come in close proximity of
sink and deliver the data.

questions.question: Which of the following is/are a challenge/challenges in Human-centric Sensing?
questions.detailed_solution:
The following are the challenges in human-centric sensing -
a. Energy of devices
b. Participant selection
c. Privacy of users

questions.question: Which of the following is/are a feature/features of UAV networks?
questions.detailed_solution:
The following are the features of UAV networks -
d. Mesh or star networks
e. Multi-tasking
f. Large coverage area

questions.question: State whether the following statement is true or false. Statement: Frequently change in network topology is a benefit of UAV networks.
questions.detailed_solution: Frequently change in network topology an issue of UAV networks.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a feature of multi-UAV system?
questions.detailed_solution:
Following are some of the features of multi-UAV system -
a. Low failures
b. High scalability
c. High survivability
d. High cost

questions.question: State whether the following statement is true or false. Statement: M2M communications are free of any human intervention.
questions.detailed_solution: M2M communications are free of any human intervention.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an M2M node type?
questions.detailed_solution:
M2M node types are as follows -
a. Low End Node
b. Mid End Node
c. High End Node

questions.question: What is the full form of M2SP in the context of Machine-to-Machine Communication?
questions.detailed_solution: M2SP stands for M2M Service Platform.

questions.question: The ______________ provides integrated services based on device collected data-sets.
questions.detailed_solution:
The M2M Application Platform provides integrated services based on
device collected data-sets.

questions.question: Which of the following is/are current challenge/challenges in IoT?
questions.detailed_solution:
Current challenges in IoT are -
a. Large scale of co-operation
b. Global heterogeneity
c. Unknown IoT device configuration
d. Semantic conflicts
questions.reference: See lecture 21 (Interoperability in Internet of Things) @ 03:41.

questions.question: When two IoT devices wish to communicate, semantic conflict in such a scenario in IoT interoperability refers to____________________.

options.text: Two devices built by the same manufacturer
Two devices having different processing and business logic
Two devices sensing the same physical parameter
Two devices having different deployment location

text: Two devices having different processing and business logic

questions.detailed_solution:
Semantic conflict refers to when different IoT devices have different processing
and business execution logic.
questions.answer_display: Two devices having different processing and business logic

questions.question: Which of the following issues needs to be addressed while solving user interoperability?
questions.detailed_solution:
All of the options given should be addressed while solving user
interoperability issues.

questions.question: State whether the following statement is true or false Statement: UMB core interoperability component is responsible for converting physical devices into virtually abstracted ones.
questions.detailed_solution: UMB-A is responsible for converting physical devices into virtually abstracted ones.

questions.question: What is the full form of UMB in IoT interoperability?
questions.detailed_solution: UMB stands for Universal Middleware Bridge.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a function available in the Servo library for Arduino ?
questions.detailed_solution:
The following are NOT functions of the Servo library for Arduino -
a. kill()
b. destroy()
c. burn()
The

questions.question: Arduino UNO Board is open source?
questions.detailed_solution: An Arduino UNO board is an open source electronic programmable board.

questions.question: Arduino Uno board accepts which type of input/inputs?
questions.detailed_solution: Arduino UNO board accepts analog and digital signals as input.

questions.question: Arduino boards are based on which of the following microcontroller/microcontrollers.
questions.detailed_solution:
Arduino board are based on the following microcontrollers -
a. ATMEGA328
b. ATMEGA32u4
c. ATMEGA2560
d. AT91SAM3X8E

questions.question: What is the clock speed of the Arduino Uno board?
questions.detailed_solution: The clock speed of Arduino Uno board is 16MHz.

questions.question: Consider the following Arduino sketch.
questions.detailed_solution: Since pin 'g' is writing digital values, it is required to set in OUTPUT mode.

questions.question: The "Verify" option in the Arduino IDE checks the code for ___________.
questions.detailed_solution: The "Verify" option in the Arduino IDE checks the code for compilation errors.

questions.question: How many pins does the DHT Digital Humidity and Temperature sensor have?
questions.detailed_solution: DHT Digital Humidity and Temperature sensor has 4 pins.

questions.question: What is the function of the delay() function in an Arduino program?

options.text: Introduces a delay for the specified time

text: Introduces a delay for the specified time

questions.detailed_solution:
The delay() function introduces a delay in the operation of the microcontroller
for the specified duration.
questions.answer_display: Introduces a delay for the specified time

questions.question: What does the following code snippet do?

options.text: Creates a variable to store the sensor value
Instructs the sensor to sense data
Creates an instance of servo to use it in the Arduino sketch

text: Creates an instance of servo to use it in the Arduino sketch

questions.detailed_solution:
The code snipped "Servo myservo;" creates an instance of servo to use in the Arduino
sketch.
questions.answer_display: Creates an instance of servo to use it in the Arduino sketch

questions.question: Python can only be used to build small applications.
questions.detailed_solution:
With open-source nature, python forms a strong backbone to build large
applications.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- I @ 3:46)

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a Python IDE?
questions.detailed_solution: Spyder and PyCharm are examples of Python IDE.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- I @ 05:12)

questions.question: State whether the following statement is true or false. Statement: To indicate different blocks of code, python follows rigid indention.
questions.detailed_solution: To indicate different blocks of code, python follows rigid indention
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- I @ 07:25).

questions.question: Which of the following is not a data-type in Python?
questions.detailed_solution:
The data-types in python are -
a. List
b. Tuple
c. Dictionary
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- I @ 15:20).

questions.question: State whether the following statement is true or false. Statement: Python allows us to read and write files.
questions.detailed_solution: Python allows us to read and write files.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- II @ 01:17)

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a mode to open a file?
questions.detailed_solution:
The four basic modes to open a file in python are -
a. Read mode
b. Write mode
c. Append mode
d. Both read and write mode
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- II @ 03:00)

questions.question: State whether the following command to install the PIL library in Linux is correct or not. sudo pip install pillow
questions.detailed_solution: The command to install the PIL library is sudo pip install pillow.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- II @ 17:40 )

questions.question: State whether the following statement is correct for creating a socket 's' in python. s = socket.socket(socket_family, socket_type, protocol=0)
questions.detailed_solution:
The statement s = socket.socket(socket_family, socket_type,
protocol=0) creates a socket in python.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- II @24:27).

questions.question: What is a Raspberry Pi?
questions.detailed_solution:
Raspberry pi is -
a. A computer on your palm
b. Single board computer
c. Low cost
d. Easy to access
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO RASPBERRY PI-I @05:00).

questions.question: Which of the following are required for a basic set up of Raspberry Pi?
questions.detailed_solution:
The following are required for a basic set up of Raspberry Pi -
a. Monitor
b. Keyboard
c. Mouse
questions.reference: See lecture INTRODUCTION TO RASPBERRY PI-I @ 11:33

questions.question: Which of the following keyboard key combination writes (i.e saves) the code to the file in nano editor ?
questions.detailed_solution: Ctrl+O writes the code to the file in nano editor.
questions.reference: See lecture INTRODUCTION TO RASPBERRY PI-II @ 10:44

questions.question: A relay module for Raspberry Pi has how many terminals ?
questions.detailed_solution: A relay module for Raspberry Pi has 3 terminals.
questions.reference: See lecture IMPLEMENTATION OF IOT WITH RASPBERRY PI-I @ 06:01.

questions.question: State whether the following statement is true or false. Statement: Adafruit provides a library to work with the DHT22 sensor.
questions.detailed_solution: Adafruit provides a library to work with the DHT22 sensor.
questions.reference: See lecture IMPLEMENTATION OF IOT WITH RASPBERRY PI-I @ 09:19

questions.question: What is the function of the close() function in python programming while handling files.

options.text: This function does not exist in python
It closes the current connection to the file and ensures that the file is free to use for other resources

text: It closes the current connection to the file and ensures that the file is free to use for other resources

questions.detailed_solution:
The close() function in python ensures that the file is free to use for other
resources.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- II @ 4:44)
questions.answer_display: It closes the current connection to the file and ensures that the file is free to use for other resources

questions.question: Where is the Raspbian image found?

options.text: It is not available on the Internet
It is available as image file from the Raspberry Pi official website
It can only be purchased from offline vendor

text: It is available as image file from the Raspberry Pi official website

questions.detailed_solution: Raspbian image can be downloaded from the Raspberry Pi official website.

questions.answer_display: It is available as image file from the Raspberry Pi official website

questions.question: DHT Sensor refers to Digital Humidity and Temperature Sensor.
questions.detailed_solution: DHT Sensor refers to Digital Humidity and Temperature Sensor.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture Implementation of IoT with Raspberry Pi- II @ 3:28)

questions.question: Fill in the blanks. __________ is a python library used for plotting the data in 2D.
questions.detailed_solution:
Matplotlib is a python library used for plotting the data in 2D. (Please
refer to lecture Implementation of IoT with Raspberry Pi- III @ 12:13)

questions.question: Fill in the blanks. __________ makes a scatter plot of the given points.
questions.detailed_solution: Scatter() makes a scatter plot of the given points.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture Implementation of IoT with Raspberry Pi- III @ 23:25).

questions.question: What are the two main challenges of SDN?
questions.detailed_solution:
Rule placement and Controller placement are the two main challenges
of SDN.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture Software Defined Networking- Part- I @ 16:49)

questions.question: In soft-timeout, all the rules are deleted from the switch.
questions.detailed_solution:
In soft-timeout, if no flow is received associated with a rule for a
particular time, the rule is deleted. In hard-timeout, all rules are deleted from the switch.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture Software Defined Networking- Part- I @ 24:57)

questions.question: Which of the following is a component of SDN?
questions.detailed_solution: Hardware switches and Flow rules are both the components of SDN.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture Software Def ined Networking- Part- I @ 15.47)

questions.question: What is introduced if a controller is down in SDN?
questions.detailed_solution:
Backup controller is introduced if a controller is down in SDN. (Please
refer to lecture Software Defined Networking- Part- II @ 8:29 )

questions.question: Southbound API is used to communicate between control layer and application layer.
questions.detailed_solution:
Southbound API is used to communicate between control layer and
infrastructure layer. (Please refer to
questions.reference: lecture 34 Software Defined Networking- Part- II @1:41).

questions.question: SDN is OpenFlow.
questions.detailed_solution: SDN is not OpenFlow. It is a technology/concept. (Please refer to
questions.reference: lecture 33 Software Defined Networking- Part- I @25:55).

questions.question: Which of the following forwards the sensed data based on the ID of the source node?
questions.detailed_solution:
ID-centric data forwarding forwards the sensed data based on the ID of
the source node.

questions.question: Does integrating SDN in IoT provide intelligent routing decisions?
questions.detailed_solution: Integrating SDN in IoT provides intelligent routing decisions.

questions.question: Are Indigo and LINC Open source?
questions.detailed_solution: Indigo and LINC are both open source.

questions.question: Which of the following is used to communicate among multiple controllers in the control layer?
questions.detailed_solution:
East-Westbound APIs are used to communicate among multiple
controllers in the control layer.

questions.question: How many requests can a controller handle through a single thread?
questions.detailed_solution:
A controller can handle 200 requests in a second through a single
thread. (Please refer to

questions.detailed_solution:
Size of the ternary content-addressable memory is limited at the switches

questions.question: Which of these is used for efficient base-station coordination for addressing inter-cell interference?
questions.detailed_solution:
Logically centralized control is used for efficient base-station coordination for
addressing inter-cell interference

questions.question: Which of these takes last k-th location instances to predict the next location in Mobi-Flow?
questions.detailed_solution:
Order-K Markov predictor takes last k-th location instances to predict
the next location in Mobi-Flow.

questions.question: What are the two components of ODIN?
questions.detailed_solution: Odin agent and master are the two components of ODIN.

questions.question: Which of the following is placed to deal with mice-flows ?
questions.detailed_solution:
Wild card rules can be placed to deal with mice-flows. (Please
refer

questions.question: Fill in the blank. ___________ refers to heterogeneous computing nodes distributed over a wide area to perform very large tasks.
questions.detailed_solution:
Grid Computing refers to heterogeneous computing nodes
distributed over a wide area to perform very large tasks.

questions.question: Which of the following is a property of cloud computing?
questions.detailed_solution:
On-demand network access, high level generalization of
computation and on-demand services are some of the properties of cloud computing.

questions.question: Which of the following statements are true about the different types of cloud? Statement I: Inter-cloud is a global cloud of clouds. Statement II: Volunteer cloud is a type of distributed cloud. Statement III: Public cloud has low security.
questions.detailed_solution:
All the three statements are correct for the cloud. Inter-cloud
is a global cloud of clouds. Volunteer cloud is a type of distributed cloud. And
also Public cloud has low security.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. ____________ means independent of device or location.
questions.detailed_solution:
Ubiquitous means independent of device or location. (Please
refer

questions.question: Fill in the blank. ____________ represents the on-demand delivery of computing infrastructure in the cloud.
questions.detailed_solution:
Infrastructure-as-a-Service represents the on-demand delivery
of computing infrastructure in the cloud.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. Hybrid cloud is a blend of ____________ cloud.
questions.detailed_solution:
Hybrid cloud is a blend of public and private cloud. (Please
refer

questions.question: Software-as-a-Service does not support a fully pay-as-you-go model.
questions.detailed_solution: Software-as-a-Service supports a fully pay-as-you-go model.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the quality of services expected by the customer?
questions.detailed_solution:
Service Level Agreement describes the quality of services
expected by the customer

questions.question: Which of the following are the accesses enabled by the access control layers in the cloud?
questions.detailed_solution:
Service access, Server access and Database access are the accesses
enabled by the access control layers in the cloud

questions.question: Reputation refers to the belief of an entity's standing by the community.
questions.detailed_solution:
Reputation refers to the belief of an entity's standing by the
community.

questions.question: Which of the following is true about GreenCloud?

options.text: GreenCloud monitors the energy consumption of servers, switches, etc
GreenCloud was developed as an extension of NS2

questions.detailed_solution:
GreenCloud monitors the energy consumption of servers,
switches, etc. It was developed as an extension of NS2.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a component of OpenStack?
questions.detailed_solution: Nova and Swift are two of the many components of OpenStack

questions.question: Where is the sensed data sent for further processing in WSNs?
questions.detailed_solution:
The sensed data is sent to sink node for further processing in WSNs (Please
refer

questions.question: Select the statement(s) that denote the advantages of cloud computing. Statement I: Elasticity Statement II: Pay-per-use Statement III: Self Service
questions.detailed_solution:
The advantages of cloud computing are Elasticity, pay-per-use and self
service

questions.question: Which of the following is a limitation of WSN?
questions.detailed_solution:
Price, post deployment maintenance and battery lifetime are the main
limitations of WSN.

questions.question: The managerial role is played by __________ in sensor-cloud architecture.
questions.detailed_solution:
Sensor-Cloud Service Provider plays the managerial role in
sensor-cloud architecture.

questions.question: The optimal composition of __________ is a management issue in sensor-cloud.
questions.detailed_solution:
The optimal composition of Virtual sensor nodes is a management
issue in sensor-cloud.

questions.question: Who coined the term Fog computing?
questions.detailed_solution: CISCO coined the term Fog computing.

questions.question: What are the issues related to the current cloud model?
questions.detailed_solution:
Volume, bandwidth and latency are some of the issues related to the current
cloud model.

questions.question: Sensor data is processed in the cloud before it is sent to the fog.
questions.detailed_solution: Sensor data is processed in the fog before it is sent to the cloud.

questions.question: What is the type of received data according to which Fog nodes work?.
questions.detailed_solution:
Fog nodes work according to the type of data which are very time-sensitive,
less time-sensitive and non-time sensitive data.

questions.question: Which of the following are the advantages of Fog?
questions.detailed_solution:
The advantages of Fog includes quick decision making, nodes
can be mobile and Fog reduces the risk of latency .

questions.question: Which of the following requires the analysis at the nearest node?
questions.detailed_solution:
Most time-sensitive data requires the analysis at the nearest
node.

questions.question: Fog nodes cannot connect and leave the network when necessary.
questions.detailed_solution: Fog nodes can connect and leave the network when necessary.

questions.question: Which of the following is used by the fog applications to increase the speed of service accessibility?
questions.detailed_solution:
Accelerators increase the speed of service accessibility. (Please
refer to Page 260, Chapter 11, Introduction to IoT. S. Misra, A. Mukherjee, and A.
Roy, 2020. Cambridge University Press.)
_________________________________________________________________________

questions.question: Fill in the blank. The concept of ________ enables physical hardware to be shared among multiple entities.
questions.detailed_solution:
The concept of hardware virtualization enables physical hardware
to be shared among multiple entities. (Please refer to Page 262, Chapter 11,
Introduction to IoT. S. Misra, A. Mukherjee, and A. Roy, 2020. Cambridge
University Press.)
___________________________________________________________________

questions.question: Smart homes can be used for _____________.
questions.detailed_solution:
Smart homes can be used for home monitoring, conservation of resources,
security and safety.

questions.question: Which of the following is an iot challenge in smart cities?
questions.detailed_solution:
Reliability, Large scale and Big data are some of the challenges of iot in smart
cities.

questions.question: Select the statement(s) that are true for smart parking. Statement I: Smart parking reduces traffic congestion Statement II: Smart parking increases urban mobility Statement III: Smart parking reduces fuel consumption
questions.detailed_solution:
Smart parking reduces traffic congestion and fuel consumption, and increases
urban mobility

questions.question: Which of the following is a functional layer in smart parking?
questions.detailed_solution:
Information Collection, System Deployment and Service Dissemination are
all the functional layers in smart parking.

questions.question: The mobile sensing in smart parking collects the information __________.
questions.detailed_solution:
The mobile sensing in smart parking collects the information
along the route.

questions.question: The network which provides amalgamation of various systems within a home is known as __________.
questions.detailed_solution:
The network which provides amalgamation of various systems within
a home is known as Home Area Network.

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct full form of DLNA Standards for HAN?
questions.detailed_solution: DLNA stands for Digital Living Network Alliance standards.

questions.question: Which of the following is a part of ZigBee Alliance?
questions.detailed_solution:
The Zigbee Alliance is composed of Mitsubishi, Honeywell, Invensys,
Motorola and Philips.

questions.question: Network and Application layers are defined by IEEE 802.15.4 in Zigbee.
questions.detailed_solution:
Network and Application layers are defined by Zigbee in Zigbee. (Please
refer

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of Content Centric Networking?
questions.detailed_solution:
Hierarchically named data, Scalable data dissemination and In-network
caching are all the features of CCN.

questions.question: Which of the following is a phase of ICV development?
questions.detailed_solution:
The phases of ICV development: Phase 1: Based on 2G, Phase
2: Based on 4G LTE, Phase 3: Vehicles connected to cloud

questions.question: Which of the following is a property of V2X?
questions.detailed_solution: V2X follows a distributed architecture.

questions.question: The vehicles cannot be mobile in the Ad-hoc domain of VANET.
questions.detailed_solution: The vehicles are mobile in the Ad-hoc domain of VANET..

questions.question: Which of the following is a domain of VANET?
questions.detailed_solution:
Both of the In-vehicle and Ad-hoc are the two domains of
VANETs.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. ICV is based on ________.
questions.detailed_solution: ICV is based on Dedicated Short Range Communication.

questions.question: What are some of the other names of the smart grids?
questions.detailed_solution:
Smart grids are also known as Electricity with a brain, Energy internet, and
Electronet.

questions.question: Traditional Grid follows which type of flow of energy?
questions.detailed_solution: Traditional grid follows unidirectional flow of energy.

questions.question: Which of the following is a benefit associated with smart grids?
questions.detailed_solution:
Smart Grids provide efficient transmission of electricity, lower
electricity rates and Improved security.

questions.question: Which of the following is a way of consumer engagement?
questions.detailed_solution:
Net metering, Time-of-Use pricing and Financial incentives are
ways of consumer engagements.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. In ___________, a home can have power from distributed resources.
questions.detailed_solution:
In islanding, a home can have power from distributed
resources.

questions.question: Which of the following aggregates energy consumption of a certain geographical area?
questions.detailed_solution:
Data Aggregator Units aggregates energy consumption of a
certain geographical area

questions.question: Which of the following statements are true about Dynamic System Attacks? Statement I: Replay attacks compromises sensors and monitors their outputs. Statement II: D-DIA can lead to a system collapse. Statement III: Covert attack is a closed loop version of replay attacks.
questions.detailed_solution:
Replay attacks compromises sensors and monitors their outputs.
D-DIA can lead to a system collapse. Covert attack is a closed loop version of replay
attacks.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. ____________ is a centralized coordinator for smart grid communication.
questions.detailed_solution:
MDMS is a centralized coordinator for smart grid
communication.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. ____________ are available protocols for smart home appliances.
questions.detailed_solution:
C-Bus, DECT, EnOcean and Universal Powerline Bus are
available protocols for smart home appliances.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. ____________ is the time required to transmit the data.
questions.detailed_solution: Latency is the time required to transmit the data.

questions.question: IIoT is based on Wrap & Replace approach.
questions.detailed_solution: IIoT is based on wrap & re-use approach.

questions.question: Which of the following happened in the 3rd Revolution?
questions.detailed_solution:
Internet evolution and automation happened in the 3rd
Revolution.

questions.question: Which of the following is used to improve the interoperability of different systems?
questions.detailed_solution:
Standardization improves the interoperability of different
systems.

questions.question: Big data is characterized by 7 Vs.
questions.detailed_solution: Big data is characterized by 7 Vs.

questions.question: What is Hadoop used in?
questions.detailed_solution:
Hadoop is used for distributed processing of large datasets across
Large clusters of computers

questions.question: The two types of data analysis are _____________.
questions.detailed_solution: Qualitative and Quantitative are the two types of data analysis.

questions.question: Which of the following is a principle of Qualitative analysis.
questions.detailed_solution:
The three principles of Qualitative analysis are: Notice things, think about
things and collect things.

questions.question: Select the statement(s) that is involved with quantitative analysis. Statement I: Data dispersion Statement II: Contingence Statement III: Correlation
questions.detailed_solution:
Data dispersion, Contingence and Correlation are involved with quantitative
analysis.

questions.question: Which of the following is a type of data dispersion?
questions.detailed_solution: Range and average absolute deviation are the type of data dispersions.

questions.question: In ANOVA, if the distribution of each group is normal, it is known as __________.
questions.detailed_solution:
In ANOVA, if the distribution of each group is normal, it is
known as normality.

questions.question: The expectation of the squared deviation of a random variable from its mean is known as __________.
questions.detailed_solution:
The expectation of the squared deviation of a random variable from its
mean is known as Variance.

questions.question: What are the two types of statistical models?
questions.detailed_solution: Complete and incomplete are two types of statistical models

questions.question: Which of the following is a type of table that displays the frequency distribution of the variables?
questions.detailed_solution:
Contingency table is a type of table that displays the frequency distribution
of the variables. It is also known as Cross tabulation and Cross tab.

questions.question: Advances in sensor and connectivity have enabled preventive care in IoT Healthcare.
questions.detailed_solution:
Advances in sensor and connectivity have enabled preventive care in IoT
Healthcare.

questions.question: Which of the following is an application of IoT in Agriculture?.
questions.detailed_solution:
Automated irrigation system, Automated weeding system and Water level
monitoring are all applications of IoT in agriculture.

questions.question: Which of the following is a property of in-place data analysis?
questions.detailed_solution:
In-place data analysis is power intensive, can be done on the
device and needs no network connection.

questions.question: Which of the following is the advantage of activity monitoring?
questions.detailed_solution:
Long term monitoring is one of the advantages of Activity
monitoring.

questions.question: Deep learning based data analysis cannot be performed on sensor data.
questions.detailed_solution:
Deep learning based data analysis can be done on sensor
data, images and videos.

questions.question: Which of the following is true for network-based data analysis?
questions.detailed_solution:
Network-based data analysis uses low power, good network
connection and can perform group-based analytics

questions.question: Which of the following is an advantage of continuous activity monitoring?
questions.detailed_solution:
Daily observation of behavior and Long term monitoring are two
advantages of continuous activity monitoring.

source_name: NPTEL - Introduction to Internet of Things

exam_pattern.practice_scoring_model: 1 mark per question inside this practice app

certification_weightage_hint.course_note: NPTEL course pages show that best-assignment aggregation varies by course, while certification commonly uses 25% assignment and 75% proctored exam weightage.
